title: Amauropelta margaretae
intro: Amauropelta margaretae, also known as the 'Margaret's Lady fern', is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae, native to New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Amauropelta margaretae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Amauropelta margaretae include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
